description_long: This factory building features a rectangular, two-story structure with a steeply pitched, dark gray tiled roof and four prominent chimneys. The exterior walls are finished in a weathered beige stucco, punctuated by rows of evenly spaced, multi-pane windows with dark frames. The front facade is distinguished by three large arched doorways framed in stone, suggesting main entrances or loading bays, and is flanked by smaller windows and doors. The roof includes several dormer windows, adding architectural interest and likely providing light to an attic or upper workspace. The building's overall appearance is utilitarian yet solid, with minimal decorative elements, emphasizing function over form. Its proportions and features are typical of early 20th-century European industrial architecture.
description: A two-story, beige stucco factory building with a steep gray tiled roof, multiple chimneys, and arched stone-framed entrances. Rows of dark-framed windows and dormers give it a classic industrial look.
description_view_0: The narrow end of the building with three vertically aligned windows and a single dormer window on the roof, showing the steep pitch and one chimney.
description_view_1: The long side of the building with five windows on each floor and two dormer windows on the roof, flanked by two chimneys.
description_view_2: The opposite narrow end, mirroring view 0 with three windows and a dormer, but the chimney is on the other side.
description_view_3: The main facade with three large arched doorways, two smaller doors, and multiple windows, plus two chimneys and two dormers on the roof.
